text:
```yaml infra-staging-egreen/workspace/ftp.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-config
data:
  SFTPGO_FTPD__BINDINGS__0__PORT: "2121"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__BINDINGS__0__CERTIFICATE_FILE: "/srv/sftpgo/certs/tls.crt"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__BINDINGS__0__CERTIFICATE_KEY_FILE: "/srv/sftpgo/certs/tls.key"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__PASSIVE_PORT_RANGE__START: "30000"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__PASSIVE_PORT_RANGE__END: "30005"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__DRIVER: "postgresql"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__NAME: "sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__HOST: "db-sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__USERNAME: "sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD: "sftpgociw8sWECsfgdd29fH2nwhkB"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__POOL_SIZE: "10"
  SFTPGO_HTTPD__BRANDING__NAME: "eGreen FTP server"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-secret
type: Opaque
data:
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD: c2Z0cGdvY2l3OHNXRUNzZmdkZDI5Zkgybndoa0I= # Base64-encoded value
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-sa
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-role
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["services"]
    verbs: ["get", "list"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-rolebinding
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: sftpgo-sa
    namespace: egreen
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: sftpgo-role
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 21
      targetPort: 2121
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp
    - port: 30000
      targetPort: 30000
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive0
    - port: 30001
      targetPort: 30001
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive1
    - port: 30002
      targetPort: 30002
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive2
    - port: 30003
      targetPort: 30003
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive3
    - port: 30004
      targetPort: 30004
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive4
    - port: 30005
      targetPort: 30005
      protocol: TCP
      name: ftp-passive5
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sftpgo
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: sftpgo
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: sftpgo-sa
      initContainers:
        - name: init-permissions
          image: busybox
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /srv/sftpgo/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sftpgo-data-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/data
              subPath: sftpgo-data
        - name: set-passive-ip
          image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
          command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              export LB_IP=$(kubectl get svc sftpgo -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip}')
              echo "SFTPGO_FTPD__FORCE_PASSIVE_IP=$LB_IP" > /srv/sftpgo/config/passive-ip.env
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/config
      containers:
        - name: sftpgo
          image: drakkan/sftpgo:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 2121
            - containerPort: 30000
            - containerPort: 30001
            - containerPort: 30002
            - containerPort: 30003
            - containerPort: 30004
            - containerPort: 30005
            - containerPort: 8080
              name: web-ui
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: sftpgo-config
            - secretRef:
                name: sftpgo-secret
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sftpgo-data-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/data
              subPath: sftpgo-data
            - name: config-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/config
            - name: certs-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/certs
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: certs-volume
          secret:
            secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
        - name: config-volume
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: sftpgo-data-volume
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: sftpgo-data-pvc

---
# Important to let the volume claim outside of the deployment to prevent delete it in the case of deployment deletion
# As it will store the ftp data we need to keep it
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-data-pvc
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sbs-default-retain
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db-sftpgo
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: db-sftpgo
  serviceName: "db-sftpgo"
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: db-sftpgo
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: postgres-sftpgo
          image: postgres:16
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: sftpgo
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: sftpgo
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: sftpgo-secret
                  key: SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD
          volumeMounts:
            - name: postgres-data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
              subPath: postgres-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: postgres-data
      spec:
        accessModes: [ "ReadWriteOnce" ]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 10Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: db-sftpgo
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    - port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: db-sftpgo
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
      protocol: TCP
      name: web-ui
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui
  namespace: egreen
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - web
  routes:
    - match: Host(`ftp.k8-test.egreen.fr`)
      kind: Rule
      middlewares:
        - name: redirect-https
      services:
        - name: sftpgo-web-ui
          port: 8080
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
  namespace: egreen
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - websecure
  routes:
    - match: Host(`ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr`)
      kind: Rule
      services:
        - name: sftpgo-web-ui
          port: 8080
  tls:
    secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls

---
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr
spec:
  dnsNames:
    - ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr
    - ftp.k8-test.egreen.fr
  secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
  issuerRef:
    name: letsencrypt-prod # Use the issuer created previously
    kind: ClusterIssuer
```

How to configure the sftpgo deployment to use the .env file created by the init container?
To configure the SFTPGo deployment to use the `.env` file created by the `init-container`, you need to ensure that the `.env` file is properly mounted into the main SFTPGo container and that the environment variables defined in the `.env` file are loaded when the container starts. Here's how you can achieve this:

### Steps to Configure the Deployment

1. **Mount the `.env` File in the Main Container**:
   Ensure that the `.env` file created by the `init-container` is mounted into the main SFTPGo container. This is already being done in your deployment using the `config-volume` volume.

2. **Set the `.env` File Path in the SFTPGo Container**:
   The SFTPGo container needs to know where to find the `.env` file. You can use the `env` field in the container spec to set the `SFTPGO_ENV_FILE` environment variable, which tells SFTPGo to load environment variables from the specified file.

3. **Update the Deployment**:
   Modify the `sftpgo` container spec in the deployment to include the `SFTPGO_ENV_FILE` environment variable.

### Updated Deployment Snippet

Here’s the updated `sftpgo` container spec with the necessary changes:

```yaml
containers:
  - name: sftpgo
    image: drakkan/sftpgo:latest
    ports:
      - containerPort: 2121
      - containerPort: 30000
      - containerPort: 30001
      - containerPort: 30002
      - containerPort: 30003
      - containerPort: 30004
      - containerPort: 30005
      - containerPort: 8080
        name: web-ui
    envFrom:
      - configMapRef:
          name: sftpgo-config
      - secretRef:
          name: sftpgo-secret
    env:
      - name: SFTPGO_ENV_FILE
        value: /srv/sftpgo/config/passive-ip.env
    volumeMounts:
      - name: sftpgo-data-volume
        mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/data
        subPath: sftpgo-data
      - name: config-volume
        mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/config
      - name: certs-volume
        mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/certs
        readOnly: true
```

### Explanation of Changes

1. **`SFTPGO_ENV_FILE` Environment Variable**:
   - The `SFTPGO_ENV_FILE` environment variable is set to the path where the `.env` file is created by the `init-container` (`/srv/sftpgo/config/passive-ip.env`).
   - This tells SFTPGo to load additional environment variables from this file.

2. **Volume Mounts**:
   - The `config-volume` is already mounted at `/srv/sftpgo/config`, so the `.env` file created by the `init-container` will be available in the main container.

### How It Works

1. The `init-container` named `set-passive-ip` creates the `.env` file (`/srv/sftpgo/config/passive-ip.env`) with the `SFTPGO_FTPD__FORCE_PASSIVE_IP` variable.
2. The `.env` file is stored in the `config-volume`, which is shared between the `init-container` and the main `sftpgo` container.
3. The `SFTPGO_ENV_FILE` environment variable in the `sftpgo` container points to the `.env` file, so SFTPGo loads the `SFTPGO_FTPD__FORCE_PASSIVE_IP` variable at runtime.

### Validation

After applying the updated deployment, you can validate that the `SFTPGO_FTPD__FORCE_PASSIVE_IP` variable is correctly set by:

1. Checking the logs of the `sftpgo` container to ensure it starts without errors.
2. Verifying that the passive IP is correctly configured by connecting to the FTP server and inspecting the passive mode behavior.

This configuration ensures that the `.env` file created by the `init-container` is properly utilized by the main SFTPGo container.
